title: Best place to get the manga
display_title: Best place to get the manga
wikitext:
[]: Best place to get the manga             Quote Post by scarmullet » Thu Jan 07, 2010 3:27 pm In a search for the manga, I found Barnes and noble has each volume (of the original) for less than $5 (except for volume 9 which is $69 [price is problebly a result of Koworu fanboyism]) They also have Angelic Days for less than $5 per volume. Is there a better place to get the manga?

[]: Re: Best place to get the manga             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 07, 2010 4:26 pm scarmullet wrote: except for volume 9 which is $69 [price is problebly a result of Koworu fanboyism]

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jan 07, 2010 5:04 pm I got vol. 9 of NGE for like $10 which is the pirece to used that my mom used to buy it for me as a gift. My borders has all of the vol's of NGE, and that's where I get mine at.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Feb 05, 2010 11:33 am POST DELETED FOR PIRACY REASONS. SEE RULES: http://wiki.evageeks.org/EvaGeeks_Forum_Rules_and_Guidelines

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Thu Feb 18, 2010 2:32 pm I got all my volumes from my local borders except for volume 9, where I had to get off GoHastings. I actually like buying my manga from cons. I look around the merchants room and see what mangas are where and who give the best deals. I bought volumes 1-12 of BECK for 100 dollars. BECK is now out of print so for 100 for one of my all time favorite series was a steal for me. Also usually the Sunday of a con is always an awesome time to buy stuff. I got volume 4 of Haruhi like 40-50% off.

[]:             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Mon Feb 22, 2010 4:45 pm Has anyone got any suggestions to buy stuff over in the UK? Mainly around Cambridgeshire X D I know its rather specific but like with only being 16 and all if its not on a online store or nearby I am kind of stuck. I know you can find literally anything online but the price for shipping it over and such can be a bit of chore. Plus having to risk buying stuff second hand and not really knowing what it is (Just recently got the Evangelion Volume 3 and turns out its the 'flipped' version which kinda sucks but I'm in position to send it back).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 22, 2010 6:05 pm The Triforce Fighter wrote: Has anyone got any suggestions to buy stuff overin the UK? Mainly around Cambridgeshire

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Feb 22, 2010 6:46 pm Well...I was able to check 'em out at the library, so yeah

[]:             Quote Post by The Triforce Fighter » Tue Feb 23, 2010 12:00 pm Hey thanks Mr.Tines, I did check out Forbidden Planet although they just seemed to have heaps of Naruto X D Next time I find myself in London though I'll defiantly check out out that 'Gosh!' place though. I did have a look in Waterstones but it was very expansive.... £8 for Volume 1 of Evangelion (considering Naruto is just £3.50 on amazon).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Feb 23, 2010 12:47 pm The Triforce Fighter wrote: check out out that 'Gosh!' place though.
